skill.name: sast-configuration
skill.description: Configure Static Application Security Testing (SAST) tools for automated vulnerability detection in application code. Use when setting up security scanning, implementing DevSecOps practices, or automating security reviews.
skill.summary: Set up and configure SAST tools like Semgrep, SonarQube, and CodeQL for automated security scanning in CI/CD pipelines.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file contains documentation examples only, not executable code. External command patterns are bash examples in markdown code blocks. SAML reference was misidentified as Windows SAM. No actual security risks detected.

content.user_title: Configure SAST tools for automated security scanning
content.value_statement: Manual SAST setup is complex and time-consuming. This skill provides ready-to-use configurations for Semgrep, SonarQube, and CodeQL to integrate security scanning into your CI/CD pipeline.
actual_capabilities: Configure Semgrep custom rules for language-specific security patterns
Set up SonarQube quality gates and security hotspots analysis
Integrate CodeQL with GitHub Advanced Security for vulnerability scanning
Create CI/CD pipeline configurations for GitHub Actions and GitLab CI
Tune rules to reduce false positives and optimize scan performance
Generate compliance-focused scans for PCI-DSS and SOC 2 requirements
limitations: Does not perform dynamic application security testing (DAST)
Cannot access private repositories without proper authentication
Requires existing CI/CD infrastructure for pipeline integration
Organization policy decisions require human approval

use_cases.title: DevSecOps Engineer
use_cases.description: Integrate SAST scanning into existing CI/CD pipelines to catch vulnerabilities before deployment. Configure blocking gates for critical findings.

use_cases.title: Security Team Lead
use_cases.description: Establish baseline security scanning across multiple repositories. Create custom rules for organization-specific security patterns and compliance requirements.

use_cases.title: Software Developer
use_cases.description: Set up pre-commit hooks to catch security issues locally before pushing code. Learn to interpret and remediate SAST findings efficiently.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Semgrep Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Semgrep for a Python project. I need basic security rules configured and want to integrate it with GitHub Actions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setting up first SAST tool

prompt_templates.title: SonarQube Quality Gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure a SonarQube quality gate for a Java Spring Boot application. Focus on security hotspots and set appropriate thresholds for blocking builds.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Rule Development
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a custom Semgrep rule to detect hardcoded API keys in JavaScript files. The rule should match common patterns like apiKey, api_secret, and Bearer tokens.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user writing custom security rules

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Tool SAST Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a defense-in-depth SAST strategy using Semgrep, SonarQube, and CodeQL together. Explain how to avoid duplicate findings and optimize scan times for a large polyglot codebase.

output_examples.input: Set up Semgrep for a Node.js project
output_examples.output: A complete .semgrep.yml configuration file with OWASP Top 10 rules, plus a GitHub Actions workflow that runs scans on pull requests and blocks merges on high-severity findings.

output_examples.input: Create a custom rule for SQL injection
output_examples.output: A Semgrep rule that detects raw SQL queries with string concatenation, with examples showing vulnerable patterns and safe parameterized alternatives.

best_practices: Start with baseline scans before enabling blocking gates to avoid disrupting development workflow
Exclude test files and generated code to reduce noise and improve scan performance
Document all rule suppressions and review them quarterly to ensure they remain valid
anti_patterns: Enabling all rules at once without tuning, causing alert fatigue and developer frustration
Scanning third-party dependencies or vendor code that you cannot modify
Ignoring false positive tuning, leading to wasted engineering time on non-issues

faq.question: Which SAST tool should I choose for my project?
faq.answer: Semgrep excels at custom rules and fast scans. SonarQube combines security with code quality. CodeQL offers deep analysis for GitHub repositories. Many teams use multiple tools for comprehensive coverage.

faq.question: How do I reduce false positives in SAST scans?
faq.answer: Tune rule sensitivity, add path exclusions for test files, create allowlists for known safe patterns, and use inline suppression comments with documented justifications.

faq.question: Should SAST scans block CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Start with warnings only, then block only critical and high severity findings after a tuning period. Medium and low severity should remain as non-blocking recommendations.

faq.question: How often should SAST scans run?
faq.answer: Run full scans on pull requests and daily scheduled scans on main branches. Pre-commit hooks can catch issues locally before pushing code.

faq.question: Can SAST replace manual code review?
faq.answer: No. SAST complements but does not replace manual review. Automated tools miss business logic flaws and context-specific vulnerabilities that human reviewers catch.

faq.question: What compliance standards does SAST support?
faq.answer: SAST helps meet PCI-DSS, SOC 2, HIPAA, and ISO 27001 requirements for secure code review. Semgrep and SonarQube offer pre-built compliance rule packs.
